questions.q: The Kakori Conspiracy involved the robbery of what?
questions.explanation: Revolutionaries looted a train carrying government money near Kakori.

questions.q: In which year did the Kakori train robbery take place?
questions.explanation: The Kakori action took place in August 1925.

questions.q: Which revolutionary organization carried out the Kakori action?
questions.explanation: The robbery was organised by the Hindustan Republican Association (HRA).

questions.q: The money looted at Kakori was being carried to which kind of destination?
questions.explanation: The train carried government treasury money, which the revolutionaries seized to fund their struggle.

questions.q: Kakori, near which the robbery occurred, is close to which city?
questions.explanation: Kakori is a town near Lucknow in the United Provinces.

questions.q: Which famous poet-revolutionary led the Kakori action?
questions.explanation: Ram Prasad Bismil was the chief leader of the Kakori robbery.

questions.q: Which of Bismil's close comrades, remembered as a symbol of unity, was also executed for the Kakori case?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan, a close comrade of Bismil, was tried and executed for the Kakori conspiracy.

questions.q: The main purpose of looting the government money at Kakori was to do what?
questions.explanation: The revolutionaries needed funds to purchase arms for their armed struggle against British rule.

questions.q: What happened to several of the leading participants in the Kakori conspiracy?
questions.explanation: Several leaders, including Bismil and Ashfaqulla Khan, were tried and hanged.

questions.q: The Kakori robbery targeted a train running on which broad route?
questions.explanation: The targeted train was travelling on the Shahjahanpur-Lucknow section of the railway.

questions.q: On what date did the Kakori train robbery take place?
questions.explanation: The Kakori action took place on 9 August 1925.

questions.q: Which number train was stopped and looted in the Kakori action?
questions.explanation: The revolutionaries stopped and robbed the 8 Down train heading toward Lucknow.

questions.q: How did the revolutionaries bring the targeted train to a halt at Kakori?
options: By blocking the line with logs
questions.explanation: One of the revolutionaries pulled the emergency chain to stop the train near Kakori.

questions.q: During the Kakori robbery, one passenger was accidentally killed. This unintended death added which charge against the accused?
questions.explanation: A passenger was accidentally shot dead, leading to a murder charge alongside dacoity and conspiracy.

questions.q: Besides Bismil and Ashfaqulla Khan, which two Kakori convicts were also sentenced to death and hanged?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Lahiri and Roshan Singh were also hanged in the Kakori case along with Bismil and Ashfaqulla Khan.

questions.q: Which key HRA leader involved in the Kakori action evaded arrest and was never caught?
questions.explanation: Chandrashekhar Azad escaped the police dragnet after Kakori and remained at large for years.

questions.q: The Hindustan Republican Association behind the Kakori action had been founded in which year?
questions.explanation: The Hindustan Republican Association was founded in 1924, the year before the Kakori robbery.

questions.q: The HRA's revolutionary aims, including the Kakori action, were partly outlined in a manifesto titled what?
questions.explanation: The HRA circulated a manifesto called 'The Revolutionary' setting out its goals of armed struggle.

questions.q: Many of the Kakori revolutionaries hailed from which town, an HRA stronghold in the United Provinces?
questions.explanation: Shahjahanpur was a centre of HRA activity and home to several Kakori participants including Bismil and Ashfaqulla Khan.

questions.q: In which year were the principal Kakori convicts executed?
questions.explanation: Bismil, Ashfaqulla Khan, Lahiri and Roshan Singh were executed in December 1927.

questions.q: The Kakori case is also sometimes referred to by which alternate name?
questions.explanation: It is also known as the Kakori Train Action or Kakori Kand.

questions.q: How many revolutionaries are traditionally said to have actually carried out the Kakori robbery?
questions.explanation: About ten revolutionaries are generally credited with executing the Kakori train robbery.

questions.q: Approximately how much money was looted in the Kakori robbery?
questions.explanation: The revolutionaries seized roughly 8,000 rupees, a modest sum that triggered a massive government crackdown.

questions.q: Rajendra Lahiri, one of the Kakori martyrs, was hanged a couple of days ahead of the others. At which jail was he executed?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Lahiri was hanged at Gonda jail on 17 December 1927, two days before the other three.

questions.q: The main Kakori trial was conducted at a special court in which city?
questions.explanation: The principal Kakori conspiracy trial was held before a special sessions court in Lucknow.

questions.q: One reason the small Kakori loot caused such alarm was that the revolutionaries also seized what symbolic government item?
options: The government treasury bags from the guard's cabin
questions.explanation: They broke open the government cash chest in the guard's van, a direct and humiliating strike at colonial authority.

questions.q: Which Kakori accused was acquitted of the major charges but later became a noted nationalist, and the brother of a famous future leader?
options: Sachindra Nath Sanyal was a key HRA founder tried separately
questions.explanation: Sachindra Nath Sanyal, an HRA founder and author of 'Bandi Jeevan', was a central figure prosecuted in connection with the conspiracy.

questions.q: After the Kakori arrests, the HRA was later reorganised in 1928 under a new name. What was it called?
questions.explanation: Following the Kakori setback, the group was reorganised as the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA) in 1928.

questions.q: Manmath Nath Gupta, a young Kakori participant, escaped the gallows but received what punishment?
questions.explanation: Being a minor at the time, Manmath Nath Gupta was spared execution and sentenced to a long prison term.

questions.q: Several accused in the Kakori case complained that their cause was weakened because some comrades turned what during the trial?
questions.explanation: The prosecution was aided when some accused turned approvers and testified for the government against their comrades.
